rows.action: Don't smoke (quit ASAP if you do)
Build cardiorespiratory fitness (≥150 min/wk zone-2 + some vigorous)
Treat high blood pressure to target
Balance training (esp. from midlife — falls/fracture prevention)
Don't sunburn / never use tanning beds
Get recommended adult vaccines (Shingrix, RSV, flu, COVID, pneumococcal)
Lift heavy for bone (HiRIT) if low BMD
Protect hearing + treat hearing loss early with aids
Correct vision / cataract surgery when it impairs you
CBT-I if you have insomnia (prevents depression)
Sleep ~7–7.5 h on a consistent schedule
Keep functional teeth (hygiene + regular dental care)
Treat OSA if symptomatic (symptoms, not MACE)

doNotBother.text: Daily aspirin for primary prevention — reversed (bleeding ≥ benefit)
Whole-body MRI / multi-cancer-early-detection (Galleri) in average-risk — overdiagnosis, no outcome data
Vitamin D / omega-3 / NAD+ / most longevity supplements — RCT-null on hard endpoints
Fasting / time-restricted eating / CR for longevity — human evidence surrogate-only
Periodontal treatment to prevent heart disease/dementia — MR-null (keep teeth for function)
Strict sun avoidance — no proven upside once the vitamin-D channel is closed; possible downside

plainLanguage: Don't smoke, and keep alcohol low. The biggest single mortality lever, with reversible benefit.
Be fit and strong — regular cardio for the heart, lifting for muscle, balance work so you don't fall and break something later.
Get blood pressure treated if it's high, and statins if your heart risk is up — the clearest causal life-extenders in medicine.
Do the screenings/vaccines that actually pay off — colorectal (and cervical) cancer screening; shingles/RSV/flu/COVID/pneumococcal vaccines.
Don't get sunburned, skip tanning beds (and wear sunscreen) — cheap, prevents the one lethal skin cancer.
Eat to avoid the clearly-bad — minimize ultra-processed food and sugary drinks, don't become obese, DASH-style for blood pressure. Don't chase superfoods, supplements, or fasting.
Protect the irreplaceable hardware — hearing (treat loss early), vision (cataract surgery), functional teeth.
Sleep ~7–7.5 h consistently; fix insomnia with CBT-I. Keep social connection and a sense of purpose — good for you, though the longevity evidence there is softer.

questions.question: Nicotine vaping: risk vs never-use and vs continued smoking? RESOLVED to T2 (patch 2026-06-18)
questions.bestGuess: << cigarettes (esp. respiratory); NOT harm-free vs never-use (CVD AOR ~1.31); "95%" is an unmeasured 2015 estimate; MI signal driven by ex-smokers
questions.revisitWhen: When a decadal hard-endpoint cohort matures

questions.question: Cannabis: CV + cognitive effects? RESOLVED to T2 (run 2026-06-18)
questions.bestGuess: Modest observational CV/mortality signal, tobacco-confounded; cognitive harm credible only for adolescent-onset heavy use; adult occasional use ≈ low measured harm

questions.question: Cannabis: does the CV/mortality signal survive proper tobacco-co-use adjustment?
questions.whyUnresolved: No meta-analysis isolates cannabis from co-smoked tobacco; no never-tobacco pooled estimate
questions.bestGuess: Independent effect likely smaller than crude RRs suggest
questions.revisitWhen: If/when a tobacco-stratified pooled analysis is published

questions.question: Cannabis: is the Dunedin adolescent-IQ decline causal or SES/genetically confounded?
questions.whyUnresolved: Rogeberg vs original authors dispute unresolved; a 2016 co-twin study (genetic confounding) wasn't adjudicated
questions.bestGuess: Real association; causal fraction uncertain. Moot for adult occasional use
questions.revisitWhen: If revisiting adolescent-specific guidance

questions.question: Cannabis: route differences (smoked vs edible vs vaporized) on hard endpoints?
questions.whyUnresolved: No head-to-head comparison verified; acute-MI data are smoking-specific
questions.bestGuess: Combustion suspected as main CV/respiratory driver
questions.revisitWhen: Low priority

questions.question: Smokeless tobacco: is heart-disease mortality truly null or modestly elevated?
questions.whyUnresolved: Key "null" study is Altria-affiliated (S006); other cohorts find HR ~1.63
questions.bestGuess: Probably modestly elevated; all-cause penalty (HR 1.36) is solid
questions.revisitWhen: If SLT becomes a personally relevant option

questions.question: Alcohol: does the 2022 GBD age-specific threshold (small net benefit for some older adults) survive Mendelian randomization?
questions.whyUnresolved: Active debate; IHD protection may be confounding/sick-quitter bias
questions.bestGuess: "Zero optimal" robust at net level; per-outcome IHD benefit least secure
questions.revisitWhen: Revisit at synthesis if alcohol ranks near a decision boundary

questions.question: Exercise: do resistance/balance TRAINING interventions move falls, fracture, BMD? RESOLVED to T1 (RCT) — targeted patch 2026-06-18
questions.bestGuess: YES, causal: balance training cuts rate of falls RaR 0.76 (Cochrane HIGH) & fractures ~−25–33% (RR 0.74); only HiRIT builds lumbar bone (+2–4%). Walking/flexibility/lifting-alone do NOT prevent falls
questions.revisitWhen: — (see exercise.md #9–#14)

questions.question: Exercise: sarcopenia muscle-MASS / strength / gait-speed intervention magnitudes?
questions.whyUnresolved: The targeted patch covered falls/fracture/BMD but the RT effect-size numbers on muscle mass, strength, chair-stand did NOT survive verification
questions.bestGuess: Direction certain (RT builds muscle/strength); pooled magnitudes un-pinned
questions.tier: predictor + falls/fracture confirmed; muscle-mass magnitudes thin
questions.revisitWhen: Low priority — doesn't change the action ("lift heavy ~2×/wk")

questions.question: Exercise: MUSCLE/strength detraining-retraining kinetics (bone kinetics now known: ~12 mo to baseline)?
questions.whyUnresolved: Only bone detraining was quantified this session; muscle/strength kinetics didn't survive
questions.bestGuess: Physiologically SLOW-reversible; benefits need maintenance, not banked
questions.revisitWhen: If reversibility weighting becomes decisive in ranking

questions.question: Exercise: is measured CRF a quantifiably stronger mortality predictor than self-reported activity, head-to-head?
questions.whyUnresolved: Data strongly suggest yes; no surviving claim makes the direct same-population comparison
questions.bestGuess: Yes — measured CRF is the cleaner signal; self-report attenuates
questions.revisitWhen: Low priority — doesn't change the action

questions.question: Exercise: resistance-training dose optimum — plateau (~90–119 min/wk) or J-curve (peak 30–60 min/wk)?
questions.whyUnresolved: Two strong cohorts disagree (Zhang S031 vs Momma-type S037)
questions.bestGuess: Effective dose is small either way (~2 sessions/wk); exact optimum unsettled
questions.revisitWhen: Low priority — both point to "~2×/wk is enough"

questions.question: Sleep: reversibility tiers (FAST/SLOW/IRREVERSIBLE) — acute-debt recovery, sleep-banking, weekend catch-up, chronic-restriction normalization?
questions.whyUnresolved: No claim survived adversarial verification across two scoped attempts
questions.bestGuess: Acute debt ≈ FAST (largely recoverable, imperfect); chronic risk ≈ SLOW; "sleep banking" weak
questions.revisitWhen: Low priority — actions don't change; revisit only if reversibility weighting becomes decisive

questions.question: Sleep: does CBT-I prevent depression in NON-elderly adults?
questions.whyUnresolved: The hard-endpoint RCT (Irwin, HR 0.51) is older-adults, single-site
questions.bestGuess: Likely generalizes; unproven
questions.revisitWhen: If CBT-I/depression ranks near a decision boundary

questions.question: Sleep: any RCT (vs observational) that improving sleep lowers hard CV events / BP / mortality?
questions.whyUnresolved: Confirmed set has none — CBT-I & sleep extension only move surrogates/depression
questions.bestGuess: Probably a true gap (cf. CPAP MACE null)
questions.revisitWhen: Watch for large sleep-intervention outcome trials

questions.question: Sleep: is short-sleep (<7h) causally harmful for mortality at all?
questions.whyUnresolved: The two biggest meta-analyses disagree (GeroScience HR 1.14 vs Kwok NS); MR-insomnia suggests some causal cardiometabolic effect
questions.bestGuess: Small real effect, smaller than observational HRs
questions.revisitWhen: At synthesis if sleep ranks near a boundary

questions.question: Nutrition: PREDIMED (the Mediterranean-diet RCT) specific numbers?
questions.whyUnresolved: Crowded out of verification across 5 runs (verify-cap) — not independently confirmed in project tiers
questions.bestGuess: Primary MACE HR ~0.69–0.72 in high-risk adults; consistent with the lever but unverified here
questions.revisitWhen: If MedDiet ranks near a decision boundary; DASH-BP already anchors causal diet

questions.question: Nutrition: does sodium REDUCTION cut hard CVD events, and the PURE very-low-sodium J-curve?
questions.whyUnresolved: DASH/sodium lower BP (surrogate) but hard-endpoint + J-curve adjudication weren't verified
questions.bestGuess: Modest benefit likely; extreme-low-sodium harm contested
questions.revisitWhen: If sodium ranks near a boundary

questions.question: Nutrition: does the Look AHEAD weight-loss CV-null generalize beyond diabetics?
questions.whyUnresolved: No RCT of intentional weight loss powered for hard endpoints exists outside T2D
questions.bestGuess: Causal weight-loss→mortality benefit genuinely untested in non-diabetics
questions.revisitWhen: Watch for new trials; affects how hard to weight "lose weight" as an action

questions.question: Nutrition: any HUMAN causal evidence for protein-restriction / mTOR longevity?
questions.whyUnresolved: Entirely rodent/mechanistic (T3); the one human cohort (Levine) is confounded & contested
questions.bestGuess: Muscle-preservation (adequate protein) beats restriction-for-longevity
questions.revisitWhen: Low priority — adequate-protein guidance is the safer default

questions.question: Nutrition: fiber dose-response shape (the 25–29 g "plateau" was refuted)?
questions.whyUnresolved: Clean threshold didn't survive verification
questions.bestGuess: Monotonic-with-attenuation, no proven ceiling
questions.revisitWhen: Low priority

questions.question: Medical: COVID-19 vaccine mortality benefit (project-tier verified)?
questions.whyUnresolved: Crowded out of verification across 3 runs; large pandemic-era benefit is established externally but not re-derived here
questions.bestGuess: Large severe-disease/mortality benefit (well-established)
questions.revisitWhen: Low priority — included in actions on external consensus

questions.question: Medical: polypharmacy→harm and what deprescribing RCTs show on hard endpoints?
questions.whyUnresolved: The "less is more" arm produced no surviving claims
questions.bestGuess: Polypharmacy assoc w/ falls/ADEs (confounded by indication); deprescribing safe, reduces inappropriate meds, limited mortality evidence
questions.revisitWhen: Useful for older-adult guidance; revisit if ranking values it

questions.question: Medical: MCED (Galleri) / whole-body-MRI quantified false-positive & overdiagnosis harms?
questions.whyUnresolved: No mortality RCT exists yet (NHS-Galleri pending); no verified claims this run
questions.bestGuess: Net-harmful in asymptomatic average-risk (incidentaloma cascade); no outcome data
questions.revisitWhen: When NHS-Galleri or outcome data matures

questions.question: Medical: below what baseline CVD risk do primary-prevention statins stop extending life?
questions.whyUnresolved: The risk threshold for negligible absolute benefit wasn't pinned
questions.bestGuess: Benefit scales with risk; marginal in low-risk young adults
questions.revisitWhen: At ranking if statins sit near a boundary

questions.question: Oral-sensory: no RCT of cataract surgery / refractive correction on dementia/falls/mortality
questions.whyUnresolved: Vision lever rests on observational + negative-control inference; 2024 metas conflict (RR 0.74 vs 0.85 NS)
questions.bestGuess: ~25% dementia reduction plausible but unproven
questions.revisitWhen: If vision ranks near a boundary

questions.question: Oral-sensory: which dementia PAF is right (hearing 8% vs ~5%; vision 19% vs 2%)?
questions.whyUnresolved: Older estimates inflated; newer cohorts lower
questions.bestGuess: Direction robust, magnitude smaller than headlines
questions.revisitWhen: Materially affects ranking of sensory levers

questions.question: Oral-sensory: does treating hearing loss cut dementia INCIDENCE (not just slow cognitive-test decline), beyond the high-risk subgroup?
questions.whyUnresolved: ACHIEVE was 3yr, surrogate endpoint, subgroup benefit only
questions.bestGuess: Likely helps at-risk; unproven for incidence/general pop
questions.revisitWhen: If hearing ranks high

questions.question: Oral-sensory: falls from hearing/vision impairment (a mortality pathway) — HRs?
questions.whyUnresolved: No hazard ratio survived verification; falls intervention evidence lives in exercise lever
questions.bestGuess: Plausible contributor
questions.revisitWhen: Low priority

questions.question: Stress-social: has ANY RCT/quasi-experiment powered for a HARD endpoint (mortality, incident CVD/dementia) of a social/loneliness intervention been run?
questions.whyUnresolved: The entire causal case is empty — all intervention RCTs measured only self-reported loneliness
questions.bestGuess: True gap; surrogate-only field
questions.revisitWhen: Watch for outcome-powered social-intervention trials; central to how high social connection can rank

questions.question: Stress-social: does transitioning OUT of loneliness/isolation recover excess risk, and over what timeframe?
questions.whyUnresolved: Only persistent-vs-never cohort contrasts exist; reversal claim was refuted
questions.bestGuess: Presumed reversible (SLOW) but unproven
questions.revisitWhen: If reversibility weighting becomes decisive

questions.question: Stress-social: why do two UK Biobank analyses disagree on whether loneliness→dementia survives depression adjustment?
questions.whyUnresolved: Depression handled as confounder vs mediator vs collider differs; isolation HR 1.34 robust, loneliness 1.03 vs 1.59
questions.bestGuess: Isolation effect more robust than loneliness; loneliness independence unsettled
questions.revisitWhen: Materially affects whether loneliness is an independent dementia risk factor

questions.question: Stress-social: is the loneliness↔depression relationship causal and in which direction?
questions.whyUnresolved: Bidirectional MR requested but not resolved in verified claims
questions.bestGuess: Likely bidirectional
questions.revisitWhen: Low-moderate priority

questions.question: Stress-social: any MR / quasi-experimental evidence that CHRONIC STRESS causally drives CVD/mortality?
questions.whyUnresolved: None surfaced; only T1 interventional evidence is a NEGATIVE finding (no RCT moves hard endpoints)
questions.bestGuess: Small real effect, mostly via standard cardiometabolic risk factors; not stress-specific
questions.revisitWhen: Central causal gap; revisit if stress ranks near a boundary

questions.question: Stress-social: do stress-reduction RCTs (MBSR/CBT/meditation) move hard endpoints in SECONDARY prevention?
questions.whyUnresolved: Verified evidence only covered primary-prevention CVD mortality (AHA/Cochrane null)
questions.bestGuess: Probably surrogate-only there too
questions.revisitWhen: Watch for cardiac-rehab stress-intervention outcome trials

questions.question: Stress-social: INTERHEART psychosocial-stress PAR for MI (~30%)?
questions.whyUnresolved: Did not survive verification this run (case-control; crowded out)
questions.bestGuess: Real but case-control; weaker than the prospective HR 1.23 (PAR 3.4%)
questions.revisitWhen: If stress ranks near a boundary

questions.question: Stress-social: does cortisol/HPA-axis dysregulation specifically predict hard endpoints, independent of cardiometabolic risk?
questions.whyUnresolved: Verified allostatic-load evidence is largely repackaged standard risk factors (tautological)
questions.bestGuess: No isolated stress-specific signal demonstrated
questions.revisitWhen: Low priority

questions.question: Stress-social (purpose): any RCT of a purpose-/positive-psychology intervention that moves a HARD endpoint?
questions.whyUnresolved: None surfaced; interventions move only surrogates (well-being/purpose/depression scales)
questions.bestGuess: Likely a true gap (same as social/stress sub-levers)
questions.revisitWhen: Trial-registry search; central to whether purpose can rank as causal

questions.question: Stress-social (purpose): does the dementia association (HR ~0.76) survive longer follow-up / landmark analyses excluding prodromal cases?
questions.whyUnresolved: UK Biobank window ~5 yr; reverse causation (preclinical dementia lowers reported purpose) not excluded
questions.bestGuess: Real but partly inflated by reverse causation
questions.revisitWhen: If purpose ranks near a boundary

questions.question: Stress-social (purpose): can the Ye 2024 MR (well-being→healthspan/lifespan) be replicated with cleaner instruments, and is directionality confirmed?
questions.whyUnresolved: Single MR; sensitivity-method claim refuted; well-being genetics overlap depression
questions.bestGuess: Supportive but not definitive causal signal
questions.revisitWhen: Watch for replication MR

questions.question: Stress-social (purpose): is the optimism benefit general or concentrated in oldest-old / female high-SES cohorts?
questions.whyUnresolved: ASPREE 77+ effect; most evidence NHS/WHI women
questions.bestGuess: Possibly age-concentrated; generalizability unclear
questions.revisitWhen: If optimism ranks near a boundary

questions.question: Sun-skin (photoaging): what fraction of extrinsic facial aging is truly UV-attributable, and how reversible?
questions.whyUnresolved: The ~80–90% (Flament 2013) figure produced NO verified claim this run
questions.bestGuess: UVA-dominant direction solid; exact fraction/reversibility unverified
questions.revisitWhen: Cosmetic/QoL — likely NOT worth a dedicated run unless QoL is weighted

questions.question: Sun-skin: how much of melanoma–UV is residual phenotype confounding (pigmentation/nevi) vs direct UV?
questions.whyUnresolved: MR instrument may proxy sun-sensitivity; full phenotype adjustment attenuated keratinocyte sunburn ORs to NS
questions.bestGuess: Real direct UV effect + some phenotype confounding
questions.revisitWhen: If melanoma weighting becomes decisive

questions.question: Sun-skin: true-rising vs overdiagnosed split in melanoma incidence — how much UV-melanoma is life-threatening vs indolent?
questions.whyUnresolved: Overdiagnosis ~50–65% of invasive cases; incidence up, mortality down
questions.bestGuess: Much of "rising melanoma" is indolent overdiagnosis
questions.revisitWhen: Affects the lever's longevity weight

questions.question: Sun-skin: UVA vs UVB relative contributions across endpoints?
questions.whyUnresolved: Not resolved this run; needed to connect harm-side to sunscreen-efficacy (Section 2)
questions.bestGuess: UVB→keratinocyte mutation; UVA→photoaging & possibly melanoma
questions.revisitWhen: When integrating Section 2 (sunscreen UVA/UVB protection differs)

questions.question: Sun-skin (protection): does sunscreen prevent melanoma beyond the single Nambour RCT?
questions.whyUnresolved: All hard-endpoint evidence is one sub-tropical Australian trial; Cochrane certainty LOW; no replication exists or is likely (RCT now arguably unethical)
questions.bestGuess: Real effect (RCT + mechanism), but unreplicated and population-specific
questions.revisitWhen: If sun-protection sits near a ranking boundary; otherwise accept as best-available

questions.question: Sun-skin (net): if vitamin D isn't the mediator, what drives any residual sun-avoidance→mortality signal?
questions.whyUnresolved: Vitamin-D channel closed (null RCTs + corrected MR); MISS is one confounded cohort
questions.bestGuess: Likely confounding (avoiders sicker) ± NO/BP/circadian; not vitamin D
questions.revisitWhen: If the sun-avoidance signal is given ranking weight

questions.question: Sun-skin (net): where is the latitude/UV-index/skin-type threshold where moderate sun flips net-positive↔net-negative?
questions.whyUnresolved: MISS is fair-skinned high-latitude; harm dominates in high-UV/darker skin
questions.bestGuess: Net-positive only at low solar intensity / fair skin
questions.revisitWhen: When writing the personalized action (depends on the reader's locale/skin type)

questions.question: Sun-skin (net): any hard-endpoint RCT of controlled sun/UV EXPOSURE (not vitamin-D supplement) on mortality/CVD?
questions.whyUnresolved: All causal tests substitute a supplement for actual sun exposure
questions.bestGuess: None exists
questions.revisitWhen: Watch literature; unlikely to be run
